messages.content: we just introduced new classes, please have them unlock based on the depth of the depth of hte deungeon floor you reach
Let me start by understanding the current state of the project and what classes exist.

Now I understand the structure. Currently, classes unlock based on the player's **best level** (character level). You want to change it so classes unlock based on the **deepest dungeon floor** reached instead. Let me check how the best level is tracked and update the system.
Got it. I'll change the unlock system so classes unlock based on the deepest dungeon floor you've reached instead of your best character level.
Now update the class definitions in constants.ts to use `requiresBestFloor` and set appropriate floor thresholds:
Now update Game.tsx — change `bestLevel` to `bestFloor`, load/save `bestFloor` from storage, and track the deepest dungeon floor reached:
Now update the `handleChange` callback to track best floor instead of best level:
Now update the class select screen to show floor info instead of level info:
Now let me also check if there's a `classCardLockedStyle` that needs to exist (I see it referenced in the JSX):
It's referenced but never defined. Let me check the end of the file to see all the styles:
The locked style is missing — that would cause errors. Let me add it:
Now let me make sure the build works:
Those storage errors were there before my changes (they're on the existing storage calls). Let me check if there's a different import pattern expected:
The HomeTab uses `appStorage`. Let me fix the Game.tsx to use `appStorage` instead:
Build is clean. Let me mark the tasks done:

All done! Here's what I changed:

- **Classes now unlock based on dungeon floor depth** instead of character level
  - **Warrior** — available from the start (floor 1)
  - **Rogue** — unlocks when you reach floor 3
  - **Ranger** — unlocks when you reach floor 5
  - **Mage** — unlocks when you reach floor 7

- The game now tracks and saves your **deepest floor reached** across runs, and the class select screen shows "Deepest floor reached" instead of "Best level reached"

- Locked classes now show "Reach floor X to unlock" so players know exactly how deep they need to go
show the locked heroes on the hero select screen
Let me check how the class select screen currently renders to understand what's happening with locked classes.

I can see the class select screen at lines 207-259. It already shows locked heroes — they appear with a "?" and "Reach floor X to unlock" text, but they're dimmed out (opacity 0.5). The locked classes are already visible on the screen.

Let me check if maybe there's a filter hiding some of them somewhere, or if there's something else going on.

The locked heroes are already showing on the class select screen — they appear in the grid with a "?" and dimmed styling. But I can see the locked cards are pretty bare — they only show the name and "Reach floor X to unlock". Let me improve them so locked heroes show more of a teaser (their description and a lock icon) so players can actually see what they'll unlock. I'll also show the class color as a hint.
Also update the locked card style to be a bit more visible — still clearly locked, but not so faded that it's hard to read:
Now let me build to make sure everything compiles:

Build is clean! Here's what the locked heroes on the class select screen now show:

- **The hero's name** in their class color (faded) with a lock icon
- **Their description** so players can get excited about what they'll unlock
- **Hidden stats** shown as "??" to tease the mystery
- **"Reach floor X to unlock"** in gold text so it stands out as a goal

Locked cards are slightly dimmed and have a darker background so they're clearly not selectable, but still readable enough to get players curious about unlocking them.
give me a store that I can access that has food/items that I can buy as I'm playing
Let me check the current game structure to understand how items, gold, and the inventory work before building the store.

I can see the locked heroes are already showing on the class select screen. They display with a lock icon, class name, description, hidden stats ("??"), and the floor requirement. Let me also check the renderer and HUD to understand where the store should be accessed during gameplay.

Now I have a clear picture of the whole system. I'll build a shop that uses gold (the `score` field) as currency, appears as a new tile on the map (a shopkeeper), and opens an overlay where you can buy food, potions, and gear. Let me also check how the dungeon generates to know where to place the shop.

Now I have a full understanding. I'll build this out. The store will:

1. Spawn a shopkeeper (`$`) on each floor in a random room
2. When you step on the shopkeeper, a shop overlay opens
3. You spend your gold to buy food, potions, scrolls, and gear
4. Stock gets better on deeper floors

Now I have the full picture. Let me implement the shop system. I'll add a shopkeeper entity to each floor, and when the player walks into the shopkeeper, a shop overlay opens where they can buy items with their gold.

First, update the types to add shop data to the game state:
Now add the shop stock generation function in entities.ts:
Now add the `spawnShop` function at the end of entities.ts:
Now update the engine to spawn the shop on each floor, and handle the player stepping on the shopkeeper:
Now update the `descend` function to spawn a shop on each new floor:
Now add a `buyItem` function and export `isAtShop` helper in the engine:
Now I need to add the `uid` import to engine.ts since `buyItem` uses it:
Good, `uid` was already imported. Now let me also add a message when the player steps on the shopkeeper. I need to update `movePlayer` to detect when the player walks onto the shop tile:
Now render the shopkeeper on the map. Update the renderer to show the shopkeeper (`$`) with a gold color and glow:
Now create the Shop UI overlay component:

Now wire the shop into Game.tsx. I need to:
1. Import the Shop component and `isAtShop`
2. Add a `showShop` state
3. Auto-open the shop when the player steps on the shopkeeper
4. Add a "Shop" button that appears when near the shopkeeper
Now update the `handleChange` callback to auto-open the shop when the player steps on the shopkeeper:
Now add the shop overlay to the game render section. Let me read the current game rendering part:
Now add the Shop overlay and a "Shop" button that appears when the player is standing on the shopkeeper:
Now add the shop button style. Let me find where to add it:
Build is clean! Let me also make sure the autoplay handles the shop correctly — I should check that the autoplay doesn't get stuck on the shopkeeper:

The autoplay looks fine — it doesn't interact with shops, it'll just walk through the shopkeeper tile naturally. The shop overlay won't auto-open during autoplay since autoplay uses `setState` directly in the timer rather than `handleChange`. That's the right behavior.

All done! Here's what the store looks like:

**On the map:**
- A shopkeeper (`$`) spawns in a random room on every floor, shown in gold with a glow

**When you walk onto the shopkeeper:**
- The shop automatically opens
- A gold `[ Shop ]` button also appears in the action bar so you can reopen it

**What you can buy:**
- **Food** — 2 random food items to keep your hunger up
- **Health Potions** — always available (Greater Health Potions appear from floor 3+)
- **Scrolls** — a random scroll (mapping or teleport)
- **Weapons** — scales with floor depth (better weapons on deeper floors)
- **Armor** — scales with floor depth
- **Rings** — appear from floor 4+ for bonus stats

**Gold = your score**, so spending wisely is part of the strategy! Items you can't afford are greyed out, and the shop shows your current gold at the top.
give me a design around progressing through death of characters in the game as a communal design

That's a really cool idea — making death feel like it contributes to something bigger for everyone. Let me think through a few different directions this could go and get your input before I start building.

Let me think through a few directions this could go. Here's what I'm imagining:

**How should death help everyone?** I have a few ideas — pick what sounds most fun:

1. **Ghost Graves** — When you die, your character leaves a gravestone on that dungeon floor. In future runs, you (and other players) can find these graves, read how that hero died, and pick up a small bonus from them.

2. **Shared Altar** — Every death donates some of your gold/XP to a community pool. All players get permanent buffs that grow as more people die (like +1 HP for every 100 total deaths across all players).

3. **Legacy Bloodline** — Each death passes down a small permanent bonus to your NEXT character. The more you die and explore, the stronger future runs start. Your death history is tracked like a family tree.

4. **All of the above** — Combine graves, shared buffs, and personal legacy into one connected system.

Which direction appeals to you most? Or if you had something else in mind, let me know!

Death Design: i like legacy bloodline quite a bit...make them offspring...have different attributes that you can pick up that you pass down to the characters...and have attributes that need to be stacked over multiple lifetimes to unlock additional powerful attributes these can be damage deaths class items, we should meet other characters in the dungeon and have dialogue choices that impact what happens and the attributes that you unlock
Death Design: Legacy Bloodline

messages.content: This is an awesome vision — a full bloodline system where every death matters and your choices shape future generations. Let me plan this out properly before building.
Great choice! This is going to be a really deep system. Let me explore the codebase to understand all the pieces I'll need to work with, then design a solid plan.
Excellent, I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Now let me design the full system.
That's an incredibly detailed design. Let me read the key files to verify a few specifics, then write up the final plan.
Now let me read the HUD to understand the layout for adding the generation indicator:
I have a thorough understanding of everything. Let me now write the final plan.

Here's what I'm planning to build:

**The Bloodline System** — Every death creates an ancestor. Your offspring inherits traits that get stronger over many lifetimes.

**20 Traits to Unlock** across 6 categories:
- **Death traits** (die enough times → HP/defense bonuses)
- **Combat traits** (kill enough monsters → attack bonuses)
- **Exploration traits** (reach deep floors → speed/items)
- **Class traits** (die as each class 3 times → class-specific legacy bonuses)
- **NPC traits** (make the right dialogue choices)
- **Dynasty traits** (play 10+ generations → big bonuses to everything)

**3 NPCs you'll meet in the dungeon:**
- A **Hermit** who shares wisdom (+defense, unlocks XP trait)
- A **Wandering Merchant** with rare trades (unlocks starting item trait)
- A **Ghost of your Ancestor** who blesses you (+attack, +healing)

**Enhanced game over screen** showing your new ancestor, any traits unlocked, and your generation count.

**Bloodline viewer** where you can see all your ancestors and progress toward each trait.

Ready to start building when you give the go-ahead!
Let me get started on this! This is a big feature so I'll work through it systematically.
Now create the traits file with all 20 traits and bloodline helpers:
Now the big one — update engine.ts. Let me read the full file to work with:
Now update the engine. First, update imports and `newGame`:
Now rewrite `newGame` to accept and apply bloodline:
Now add run stat tracking to `attackEntity`. Let me find it:
Now add run stat tracking to `attackEntity`:
Now add kill tracking to the monster death section:
Now add run stat tracking to `useItem` (potions, food, scrolls):
Now add item pickup and gold tracking to `movePlayer`, and add NPC collision check:
Now update `descend` to track floors and spawn NPCs:
Actually, that approach with inline default bloodline is messy. Let me use a cleaner approach — store a bloodline reference on GameState:
Now update `newGame` to store the bloodline ref, and clean up `descend`:
Now fix the descend function to use the cleaner approach:
Now add the `applyDialogueEffects` and `extractCauseOfDeath` exported functions:
Now add the dialogue effects and cause-of-death extraction functions:
Now add the `findItemTemplate` helper to entities.ts:
Now update renderer.ts to show NPCs:
Now create the NPC dialogue overlay and bloodline view overlay in parallel:
Now the biggest file — Game.tsx. Let me read the current state to apply all changes:
Now I need to rewrite Game.tsx with all the bloodline integration. This is a major update:
Now update HUD.tsx to accept and show generation:
Now update autoplay to avoid NPC tiles:
Easy fix — need to narrow the type properly in NPCDialogue.tsx:

Everything is built and working! Here's the full Legacy Bloodline system I just added:

---

**The Offspring System**
- Every time you die, your character becomes an **ancestor**. They get a generated name like "Kira the Swift" and join your bloodline history (last 5 tracked).
- Your next character is their **offspring** — Generation 2, 3, 4, and so on.
- The generation counter shows in the HUD during gameplay (`G:5`).

**20 Traits to Unlock Across Multiple Lifetimes**
- **Death traits** — Die enough times to earn HP and defense bonuses (1, 5, 10, 25 deaths)
- **Combat traits** — Kill enough monsters for attack bonuses (10, 50 kills + 3 Dragon kills)
- **Exploration traits** — Reach deep floors, find items, eat food for speed/hunger/gold bonuses
- **Class legacy traits** — Die 3 times as each class to unlock a permanent bonus for ALL classes
- **NPC traits** — Make the right dialogue choices to unlock XP bonuses and starting items
- **Dynasty traits** — Play 10+ or 25+ generations for massive stat boosts

**3 NPCs You'll Meet in the Dungeon**
- **The Hermit** (`H`, blue, floor 2+) — Listen to gain +1 Def and unlock the XP trait
- **Wandering Merchant** (`M`, gold, floor 3+) — Trade 10 gold for a Strength Potion and unlock the starting items trait
- **Ghost of Your Ancestor** (`&`, purple, floor 5+, requires generation 1+) — References your actual last ancestor by name. Choose a blessing (+2 Atk, +10 HP) or pay respects (+30 hunger)

**Enhanced Game Over Screen**
- Shows your new ancestor's name and class
- Highlights any new traits you just unlocked
- Shows generation count and trait progress

**Bloodline Viewer**
- Accessible from the title screen and game over screen via `[ Bloodline ]` button
- **Ancestors tab** — Shows your last 5 ancestors with their class, floor, cause of death, and kill count
- **Traits tab** — All 20 traits organized by category, with progress bars for locked ones (e.g., "37/50 kills")
where can I see this in the game

The bloodline system is fully active right now! Here's where you'll see everything:

**During gameplay:**
- The **HUD** shows `G:1`, `G:2`, etc. next to the floor number once you've died at least once
- **NPCs** appear on the map as glowing characters — `H` (blue Hermit on floor 2+), `M` (gold Merchant on floor 3+), `&` (purple Ancestor Ghost on floor 5+). Walk into them and a dialogue pops up with choices

**When you die:**
- The **game over screen** now shows a "BLOODLINE" section with your new ancestor's name (like "Kira the Swift"), and any new traits you just unlocked are highlighted

**Title screen:**
- Once you've died at least once, you'll see your generation count and a purple `[ Bloodline ]` button
- Tap it to see your **ancestors list** and all **20 traits** with progress bars

**Class select screen:**
- Shows your generation, trait count, and active bonuses (like "+2 HP +1 Atk") below the floor info

**To experience it:** Just play a run and die — your first death will unlock the "First Fall" trait (+2 HP), create your first ancestor, and start Generation 1. Each run after that, your offspring starts a little stronger based on what traits you've unlocked!
Add bloodline as a button on the main screen
I see — the Bloodline button on the title screen currently only shows after you've died at least once. Let me make it always visible so you can check it out from the start.

Done! The `[ Bloodline ]` button now always shows on the main screen right next to the Leaderboard button — even before your first death. That way you can tap it anytime to see your ancestors, traits, and progress.
show me a complete list of bloodlines on the bloodlines screen
Let me check what the bloodline screen currently shows to understand what needs to change.
I see — the Traits tab already shows all 20 traits, but I'll make it the default tab and improve the layout so you see the full list right away with clear effect descriptions for each trait.
Just an unused variable — let me remove it.

Done! The Bloodline screen now:

- **Opens to Traits first** — so you immediately see all 20 traits organized by category
- **Each trait shows three lines**: name, description, and the exact effect (like "+2 Def" or "Start with Health Potion")
- **Progress bars** on every trait with numbers (e.g., "3/10 kills") so you always know how close you are
- **NPC traits** show "???" since they unlock through dialogue choices, not numbers
- **Unlocked traits** are highlighted with a glowing icon and "UNLOCKED" tag
- **Active bonuses summary** at the top shows your total inherited bonuses
- **Ancestors tab** now also shows more lifetime stats (damage dealt, damage taken, items found, food eaten)
i can't read any of the traits, please make the brighter but put a lock on them
I see the issue — the locked trait colors are way too dark (`#2a2a3a`, `#1a1a2a`, `#4a4a5a`). I'll brighten them all up and add a lock icon.

Done! Locked traits are now much more readable:

- **Lock icon** (🔒) replaces the trait icon on locked ones
- **Trait name** is now a bright light blue/purple (`#aaaacc`) instead of barely visible
- **Description** is a readable grey-purple (`#8888aa`)
- **Effect text** is a visible muted purple (`#6a6a8a`)
- **Progress numbers** are brighter too (`#8a7aaa`)

Unlocked traits still pop with their full color icon, green name, and "UNLOCKED" tag — so you can clearly tell the difference.
can you make the locks in ascii style?
Now update the font size to fit the ASCII lock nicely since `[x]` is 3 chars:
Now do the same for the locked heroes on the class select screen:

Done! Replaced all emoji locks with ASCII-style `[x]` — both on the locked traits in the Bloodline screen and on the locked heroes on the class select screen. Keeps everything consistent with the monospace terminal aesthetic.

Here's your link: **https://omw.run/u/TnwiXCIVrrnLXVcED65D/development**

You can also scan the QR code above to open it on your phone. Play a run, die, and you'll see the bloodline system kick in!

i need a multiplayer mode that is a necropolis where communal unlocks happen with cumulative player deaths. these unlocks are items, enemies dungeons, classes, weapons, armor and completely new zones that the players are unlocking by playing and dying. I need a necropolis button on the main screen. Also make food more affordable
Let me explore the codebase to understand the current systems and what SDK features are available for multiplayer/shared storage.
